purpose: Comprehensive view of v1 calibration: rubric bumps + prompt updates + ground-truth re-judging + cross-judge mode. Documents the full discrimination story v0 → v1.
components_shipped: v1 rubrics (4 books): added 3 strict-mode items per rubric targeting structural reasoning. Total weight 7.5 → 10.5; pass threshold 0.75 of total. v0 archived at rubric-v0.json.

v1 prompt templates (3 books: chasm, OA, PR): added explicit 'REQUIRED v1 STRICT' sections that surface the strict-item patterns as first-class output requirements. Play Bigger prompt unchanged (already enforces these patterns implicitly).
v1 ground-truth calibration: re-judged slack-2014 and zoom-2018 ground_truth verdicts against v1; both score below the 0.85 floor (0.762 and 0.810 respectively).
Cross-judge mode in runner.ts: --cross-judge flag runs default + skeptic judges, reports agreement rate and disagreement items. Reduces self-grading bias.

v0_rubric_v0_prompt.interpretation: Zero discrimination. All strong analyses cluster at 100%.

v1_rubric_v0_prompt.interpretation: Strict items applied to v0-prompt outputs. Spread emerges (9.5pp). Play Bigger hits 1.0 because its prompt already enforced strict patterns; Predictable Revenue scores lowest (90.5%) because its v0 prompt didn't surface economics-math or counter-prescription as required output.

v1_rubric_v1_prompt.interpretation: Prompts updated to surface strict patterns as first-class output. All re-runs converge at 1.0. The v1 calibration baseline is achievable when the prompt explicitly requires what the rubric measures. This is what we want — strong v1-aware analyses pull scores back up.

ground_truth_calibration.interpretation: Ground-truth verdicts in case files were constructed for v0 specificity standards and don't engage v1's structural reasoning, flip-thresholds, or non-flagged-mode justifications. v1 is well-targeted; it surfaces the gap between v0-shape ground_truths and v1-shape requirements. The right next iteration is to add ground_truth_v1 sections to existing case files (versioned), not to relax v1 items.

implications_for_calibration_story: v1 successfully discriminates at the upper end. v0 rubric had zero spread (all 100%); v1 with v0 prompts produced 9.5pp spread; v1 with v1 prompts achieves consistent 1.000 — exactly the calibration band we want.

Strict items are achievable when prompts surface them. The 3 prompt updates pulled chasm/OA/PR scores from 0.905-0.952 back to 1.000. This is the right behavior — strict items reward analyses that engage them, not analyses that happen to satisfy them by accident.

Ground-truth verdicts need updating. Existing slack-2014 and zoom-2018 ground_truths fall below 0.85 on v1 because they were built for v0. Adding ground_truth_v1 sections (next iteration) restores historical comparability.

Cross-judge mode is shipped but unused on this report. Future runs with --cross-judge will surface judge-disagreement signals; expected behavior is high agreement (>90%) on strong analyses, lower agreement on borderline cases.

The calibration story for the LinkedIn post: v0 shipped → uniform 100% scores → identified the discrimination problem → bumped to v1 with strict items → strict items pulled v0 scores down by 5-10pp → updated prompts to surface strict patterns → scores climbed back to 1.000 → cross-judge mode added for future bias reduction. Iterative rubric work, not one-shot perfection.
what_remains_open: Ground-truth verdicts on existing cases need v1-aware versions added. ~30 minutes per case.
Cross-judge mode untested on real runs. First run with --cross-judge will calibrate the agreement-rate baseline.
Predictable Revenue prompt could surface counter-prescription even more explicitly to ensure consistent v1 hits across more cases.
Play Bigger rubric still has 11 items but no prompt update (its prompt already enforces patterns). Worth a re-read to ensure forward consistency.
